key_insight: The paper introduces a polyconvex PANN constitutive model for anisotropic hyperelasticity, addressing unresolved challenges in material theory.
engineering_relevance: This work enhances the predictive capabilities of constitutive models in engineering applications involving complex material behaviors.
method: Proposes a new polyconvex PANN constitutive model using triclinic invariants and group symmetrization.
Develops a method for constructing polyconvex invariants applicable to finite symmetry groups.
Derives integrity and functional bases for tetragonal and cubic symmetry groups.
evidence_signal: The models are benchmarked against highly nonlinear homogenization data of cubic metamaterials.
watch_item: Potential limitations in the applicability of the proposed models to materials with higher complexity or non-standard geometries.
